name: Andouille a La Jeannine
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the andouille into 1/4- to 1/2-inch thick slices.
2. Mix the dry white wine, honey, and creole mustard in a bowl until combined.
3. Pour the mixture over the sliced andouille in a covered skillet.
4. Cook over low heat until the andouille is tender.
Andouille is gumbo sausage for all you people who live away from the center of the universe. You can use other sausages, and it would taste okay.
